title: claytonia perfoliata var. viae
intro: Claytonia perfoliata var. viae, also known as the miner's lettuce, is a type of flowering plant in the portulacaceae family. It is a succulent perennial that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals such as calcium, iron, and potassium, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting bone health. Herbal preparations of Claytonia perfoliata var. viae can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues.
